decks.rationale: Exponential passive RC smoke deck used to keep alias-style `EXP(low=... high=... delay1=... tau_rise=... delay2=... tau_fall=...)` source parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Exponential passive RC smoke deck used to keep comma-separated keyword-style `EXP(v1=..., v2=..., td1=..., tau1=..., td2=..., tau2=...)` source parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Exponential passive RC smoke deck used to keep case-insensitive source-function parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned for mixed-case `eXp(v1=... v2=... td1=... tau1=... td2=... tau2=...)` keyword spelling in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Exponential passive RC smoke deck used to keep keyword-style `EXP(v1=... v2=... td1=... tau1=... td2=... tau2=...)` source parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Exponential passive RC smoke deck used to keep optional whitespace between the function name and `(` aligned across native parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering for `EXP (v1=... v2=... td1=... tau1=... td2=... tau2=...)` keyword spelling in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Exponential passive RC smoke deck used to keep case-insensitive source-function parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned for mixed-case `eXp(...)` spelling in the thresholded waveform baseline.
Exponential passive RC smoke deck used to keep rise-and-fall source shaping in the first thresholded waveform-source baseline.

Exponential passive RC smoke deck used to keep optional whitespace between the function name and `(` aligned across native parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering for `EXP (...)` in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Chained-include JJ deck with an internal delayed-T source path in the leaf included file; keeps nonlinear model lookup and transport-delay semantics coupled under include-of-include hierarchy flattening.

Chained-include JJ deck with an internal delayed-T path driven by a file-driven PWL source in the leaf included file; keeps nonlinear model lookup, transport-delay semantics, and source-file locality coupled under include-of-include hierarchy flattening.

Chained-include JJ deck with an internal delayed-T path driven by a file-driven PWL source and feeding a mutual-inductor path in the leaf included file; keeps nonlinear model lookup, transport-delay semantics, mutual-coupling semantics, and source-file locality coupled under include-of-include hierarchy flattening.

Chained-include delayed-T deck with a file-driven PWL source used to keep transport-delay behavior and source-file locality coupled under include-of-include hierarchy flattening inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Chained-include delayed-T subckt smoke deck used to keep transport-delay behavior under include-of-include hierarchy flattening inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Chained-include JJ deck with the model card defined in the leaf included file; keeps nonlinear model lookup and include-of-include hierarchy flattening inside the JJ baseline.
Chained-include mutual-inductor smoke deck used to keep coupling behavior under include-of-include hierarchy flattening inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Chained include passive RC smoke deck used to keep include-of-include expansion and file-backed hierarchy assembly inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Chained-include JJ deck with an internal pulse source in the leaf included file; keeps nonlinear model lookup and source-bearing include-of-include hierarchy coupled inside the JJ baseline.

Chained-include JJ deck with an internal source-bearing mutual-inductor path in the leaf included file; keeps nonlinear model lookup and mutual-coupling semantics coupled under include-of-include hierarchy flattening.
Chained-include mutual-inductor deck with an internal pulse source used to keep coupling behavior and source-bearing include-of-include hierarchy coupled inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.

Chained-include JJ deck with an internal file-driven PWL source in the leaf included file; keeps nonlinear model lookup and source-file locality coupled under include-of-include hierarchy flattening inside the JJ baseline.

Chained-include JJ deck with an internal file-driven source-bearing mutual-inductor path; keeps nonlinear model lookup, mutual-coupling semantics, and source-file locality coupled under include-of-include hierarchy semantics.
Chained-include file-driven PWL source subckt smoke deck used to keep include-of-include source-file locality inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Included subckt JJ deck with an internal delayed-T source path; keeps nonlinear model lookup and transport-delay semantics coupled inside file-backed hierarchy semantics.

Included subckt JJ deck with an internal delayed-T path driven by a file-driven PWL source and feeding a mutual-inductor path; keeps nonlinear model lookup, transport-delay semantics, mutual-coupling semantics, and source-file locality coupled inside file-backed hierarchy semantics.

Included subckt delayed-T deck with a file-driven PWL source used to keep transport-delay behavior and source-file locality coupled under file-backed hierarchy flattening inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Included subckt T-line smoke deck used to keep transport-delay behavior under file-backed hierarchy flattening inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Included subckt JJ deck with a model card defined in the included file; keeps file-backed model resolution and hierarchy flattening inside the first nonlinear JJ baseline.
Included subckt mutual-inductor smoke deck used to keep coupling behavior under file-backed hierarchy flattening inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Included subckt JJ deck with an internal source-bearing mutual-inductor path; keeps nonlinear model lookup and mutual-coupling semantics coupled inside file-backed hierarchy semantics.
Included subckt mutual-inductor deck with an internal pulse source used to keep coupling behavior and source-bearing hierarchy coupled inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Included subckt JJ deck with an internal file-driven PWL source; keeps nonlinear model lookup and source-file locality coupled inside file-backed hierarchy semantics.

Included subckt JJ deck with an internal file-driven source-bearing mutual-inductor path; keeps nonlinear model lookup, mutual-coupling semantics, and source-file locality coupled inside file-backed hierarchy semantics.
Included file-driven PWL source subckt smoke deck used to keep source-file locality inside file-backed hierarchy semantics in the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Included source-bearing subckt passive RC smoke deck used to keep source flattening inside file-backed hierarchy semantics in the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Included subckt passive RC smoke deck used to keep file-backed include expansion and parameterized subckt flattening inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises supported fifth-harmonic CPR lowering through `.model ... jj(...)` defaults while also overriding `rn` at the instance site, so the numeric phase-6 baseline covers the current benchmark-safe model-default-plus-inline-override merge path for five-coefficient JoSIM `CPR={...}` emission without dropping harmonic semantics.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises newly supported fifth-harmonic CPR lowering through a `.model ... jj(...)` card plus an instance-side `icrit5` override, so the numeric phase-6 baseline now covers five-coefficient JoSIM `CPR={...}` emission through alias lowering instead of stopping at fourth harmonic.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises pure fifth-harmonic semantics without any primary `icrit` term, so the numeric phase-6 baseline starts covering native JoSIM `CPR={0,0,0,0,1}` emission when the fifth harmonic is the only available basis current.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises supported fourth-harmonic CPR lowering through `.model ... jj(...)` defaults while also overriding `rn` at the instance site, so the numeric phase-6 baseline covers the current benchmark-safe model-default-plus-inline-override merge path for four-coefficient JoSIM `CPR={...}` emission without dropping harmonic semantics.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises newly supported fourth-harmonic CPR lowering through a `.model ... jj(...)` card plus an instance-side `icrit4` override, so the numeric phase-6 baseline now covers four-coefficient JoSIM `CPR={...}` emission instead of stopping at third harmonic.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises pure fourth-harmonic semantics without any primary `icrit` term, so the numeric phase-6 baseline starts covering native JoSIM `CPR={0,0,0,1}` emission when the fourth harmonic is the only available basis current.
Bootstrap nonlinear JJ deck using inline instance parameters; threshold remains looser than passive cases because internal JJ semantics are still partial relative to JoSIM.

Top-level JJ deck that references a `.model ... jj(...)` card through `model=...`, used to keep the external JoSIM translator aligned on keyword-style model references instead of silently falling back to default JJ semantics.

Top-level JJ deck that references a `.model ... jj(...)` card and overrides `rn` at the instance site, used to keep the external JoSIM translator aligned on the supported model-default-plus-inline-override subset instead of falling back to default model semantics.

Top-level JJ deck that references a `.model ... jj(...)` card through `modelname=...`, used to keep the internal parser and external JoSIM translator aligned on the alternate keyword-style model reference path instead of only covering `model=...`.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises native instance-side `J... cpr={...}` input through five coefficients with inline `icrit`/`rn`/`cj`, so the numeric phase-6 baseline now starts covering fifth-harmonic JoSIM-style CPR syntax on junction instances instead of stopping at the fourth term.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises native instance-side `J... cpr={...}` input through four coefficients with inline `icrit`/`rn`/`cj`, so the numeric phase-6 baseline now covers direct JoSIM-style fourth-harmonic CPR syntax on junction instances instead of stopping at the third harmonic.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises native instance-side `J... cpr={...}` input with inline `icrit`/`rn`/`cj` through the first three harmonic coefficients, so the numeric phase-6 baseline covers direct JoSIM-style CPR syntax on junction instances instead of only model-card defaults.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises native `.model ... jj(... cpr={...})` input through five coefficients, so the numeric phase-6 baseline now starts covering fifth-harmonic JoSIM-style CPR syntax on model cards instead of stopping at the fourth term.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises native `.model ... jj(... cpr={...})` input through four coefficients, so the numeric phase-6 baseline now covers direct JoSIM-style fourth-harmonic CPR syntax on model cards instead of stopping at the third harmonic.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises native `.model ... jj(... cpr={...})` input through five coefficients while also overriding `rn` at the instance site, so the numeric phase-6 baseline covers the supported model-default-plus-inline-override merge path for full five-coefficient native JoSIM CPR syntax without degrading model-card defaults.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises native `.model ... jj(... cpr={...})` input through the first three harmonic coefficients, so the numeric phase-6 baseline covers direct JoSIM-style CPR syntax instead of only alias-based `icrit2`/`icrit3` lowering.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises supported `pi` sign-flip semantics through both a `.model ... jj(...)` card and an instance-side `pi=1` override, used to keep the external JoSIM translator aligned on preserved pi-junction behavior instead of degrading it to warnings.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises nonzero integer `pi` spellings through a `.model ... jj(...)` default of `pi=-1` plus an instance-side `pi=2` override, so the numeric phase-6 baseline now covers the real JoSIM-compatible integer forms that the translator and internal parser normalize into sign-flip semantics.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises supported second-harmonic CPR lowering through `.model ... jj(...)` defaults while also overriding `rn` at the instance site, so the numeric phase-6 baseline covers the current benchmark-safe model-default-plus-inline-override merge path without dropping harmonic CPR semantics.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises supported second-harmonic CPR lowering through a `.model ... jj(...)` card plus an instance-side `icrit2` override, used to keep the external JoSIM translator aligned on native JoSIM `CPR={...}` emission instead of degrading second-harmonic semantics to warnings.

Top-level JJ deck that now exercises pure second-harmonic semantics without any primary `icrit` term, using a tighter transient step so both internal transient and external JoSIM can keep this previously warning-only boundary inside the numeric phase-6 baseline.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises supported sixth-harmonic CPR lowering through `.model ... jj(...)` defaults while also overriding `rn` at the instance site, so the numeric phase-6 baseline covers the current benchmark-safe model-default-plus-inline-override merge path for six-coefficient JoSIM `CPR={...}` emission without dropping harmonic semantics.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises newly supported sixth-harmonic CPR lowering through a `.model ... jj(...)` card plus an instance-side `icrit6` override, so the numeric phase-6 baseline now covers six-coefficient JoSIM `CPR={...}` emission through alias lowering instead of stopping at fifth harmonic.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises pure sixth-harmonic semantics without any primary `icrit` term, so the numeric phase-6 baseline starts covering native JoSIM `CPR={0,0,0,0,0,1}` emission when the sixth harmonic is the only available basis current.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises supported third-harmonic CPR lowering through `.model ... jj(...)` defaults while also overriding `rn` at the instance site, so the numeric phase-6 baseline covers the current benchmark-safe model-default-plus-inline-override merge path for three-coefficient JoSIM `CPR={...}` emission without dropping harmonic semantics.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises newly supported third-harmonic CPR lowering through a `.model ... jj(...)` card plus an instance-side `icrit3` override, so the numeric phase-6 baseline covers three-coefficient JoSIM `CPR={...}` emission instead of stopping at second harmonic.

Top-level JJ deck that exercises pure third-harmonic semantics without any primary `icrit` term, so the numeric phase-6 baseline starts covering native JoSIM `CPR={0,0,1}` emission when the third harmonic is the only available basis current.
Mutual-inductor smoke deck used to catch coupling regressions without overfitting the current linear transient approximation.
Library-section passive RC smoke deck used to keep `.lib <path> sec =...` tokenization (value attached to the equals token) aligned in the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Library-section passive RC smoke deck used to keep `.lib/.endl sec =...` library-block header tokenization (value attached to the equals token) aligned in the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Library-section passive RC smoke deck used to keep `.lib <path> sec = ...` alias selection and section-scoped hierarchy assembly inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Library-section passive RC smoke deck used to keep `.lib <path> section= ...` tokenization (empty right-hand side with value in the next token) aligned in the thresholded phase-6 baseline.

Library-section passive RC smoke deck used to keep `.lib/.endl section= ...` library-block header tokenization (empty right-hand side with value in the next token) aligned in the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Library-section passive RC smoke deck used to keep `.lib <path> section = ...` selection and section-scoped hierarchy assembly inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.
Nested included subckt passive RC smoke deck used to keep parameter passthrough across flattened hierarchy inside the thresholded phase-6 baseline.

Pulse-driven passive RC smoke deck used to keep case-insensitive source-function parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned for mixed-case `pUlSe(v1=... v2=... td=... tr=... tf=... pw=... per=... ncycles=...)` keyword spelling in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Pulse-driven passive RC smoke deck used to keep keyword-style `PULSE(v1=... v2=... td=... tr=... tf=... pw=... per=... ncycles=...)` source parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Pulse-driven passive RC smoke deck used to keep optional whitespace between the function name and `(` aligned across native parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering for `PULSE (v1=... v2=... td=... tr=... tf=... pw=... per=... ncycles=...)` keyword spelling in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Pulse-driven passive RC smoke deck used to keep case-insensitive source-function parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned for mixed-case `pUlSe(...)` spelling in the thresholded waveform baseline.
Pulse-driven passive RC smoke deck used to keep a first waveform-source baseline in the JoSIM correlation gate without requiring JJ-specific semantics.

Pulse-driven passive RC smoke deck used to keep optional whitespace between the function name and `(` aligned across native parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering for `PULSE (...)` in the thresholded waveform baseline.
File-driven PWL passive RC smoke deck used to keep external waveform-file resolution and source playback inside the first thresholded waveform-source baseline.

Sinusoidal passive RC smoke deck used to keep alias-style `SIN(offset=... amplitude=... f=... td=... damping=... phi=...)` source parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Sinusoidal passive RC smoke deck used to keep comma-separated keyword-style `SIN(vo=..., va=..., freq=..., td=..., theta=..., phi=...)` source parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Sinusoidal passive RC smoke deck used to keep case-insensitive source-function parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned for mixed-case `sIn(vo=... va=... freq=... td=... theta=... phi=...)` keyword spelling in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Sinusoidal passive RC smoke deck used to keep keyword-style `SIN(vo=... va=... freq=... td=... theta=... phi=...)` source parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Sinusoidal passive RC smoke deck used to keep optional whitespace between the function name and `(` aligned across native parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering for `SIN (vo=... va=... freq=... td=... theta=... phi=...)` keyword spelling in the thresholded waveform baseline.

Sinusoidal passive RC smoke deck used to keep case-insensitive source-function parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering aligned for mixed-case `sIn(...)` spelling in the thresholded waveform baseline.
Sinusoidal passive RC smoke deck used to keep the first continuous-wave source baseline in the same correlation gate as the pulse-driven source path.

Sinusoidal passive RC smoke deck used to keep optional whitespace between the function name and `(` aligned across native parsing and external JoSIM deck lowering for `SIN (...)` in the thresholded waveform baseline.
Single T-line smoke deck; keep a loose first-pass transport-delay threshold until delayed-line semantics mature further.
